title: kubectl Discovery, Contexts, Queries, Diff, Apply, and Safe Mutation · Cluster Case: K8s Get Describe Structured Output
summary: An operator has two similarly named clusters and a manifest owned by multiple field managers; a convenient imperative edit could hit the wrong authority and steal fields. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s get describe structured output increment in safe kubectl investigation and apply record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose get, describe, events, logs, JSONPath, custom columns, selectors, and raw JSON or YAML without parsing human tables as stable interfaces.
Use API discovery, api-resources, api-versions, explain, raw endpoints, and versioned reference material to identify authoritative fields.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-kubectl-contexts-declarative-5
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-kubectl-contexts-declarative-5
    artifact: kubectl-plan.yaml
    decision: worked k8s kubectl contexts declarative 5
    environment: managed canary cluster with provider-specific load balancing
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      context: training-cluster
      namespace: intake
      manager: learn-it-all
      preview: server-dry-run-and-diff
      mutation: server-side-apply
      delete: uid-precondition
    verify: kubectl config current-context && kubectl diff --server-side -f release.yaml
    failure: Reject implicit context, human-table parsing, client dry run as admission proof, unscoped prune, or field ownership theft.
    repair: Name cluster and namespace, use discovery and structured output, preview server behavior, scope ownership, and verify changed status.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
